rationales.self_direction: He is drawn to the smaller offer because the larger one means others 'dictating my calendar' and says that boxed-in feeling signals betraying 'how I want to live.'
rationales.security: He is willing to reject the 'obvious' bigger check and its added capital in order to avoid oversight, choosing autonomy over the safer, more predictable option.

rationales.self_direction: The line 'Took the smaller investment' follows through on choosing the hands-off path rather than accepting outside control.
rationales.hedonism: He immediately 'celebrated with Layla at that new Persian restaurant,' treating pleasure and enjoyment as part of the decision's payoff.

rationales.self_direction: He rejects being told when to marry or 'settle down' and says 'I'll figure that out when I figure it out,' affirming his own timetable.
rationales.hedonism: He explicitly defends 'Weekends in Oman. Good wine. A nice apartment' and says 'I don't want' a life built on deferring enjoyment.
rationales.tradition: He pushes back against his mother's model where 'you sacrifice everything for family, for the future,' refusing that inherited script as true for him.

rationales.hedonism: He admits he 'used to be more fun' and hates becoming the person who's always 'busy,' showing work has crowded out enjoyment and presence.
rationales.achievement: He has been 'grinding harder than usual' to prove taking the smaller check was right and to show he can grow the company.
rationales.benevolence: The conflict with Layla centers on his work consuming him, and he recognizes he needs to 'actually be present' with her because he has been neglecting the relationship.

rationales.self_direction: He frames the trip as 'why I built my own thing' and values being able to disappear for three days 'without asking permission.'
rationales.hedonism: Leaving the laptop behind, hiking to the pool, and floating in silence are all embraced as the kind of pleasurable life the business is supposed to serve.
He says yes to every request even though it means 'meetings and deadlines and no room to breathe,' sacrificing enjoyment and recovery.
rationales.achievement: He accepts all three client requests because he is 'still in growth mode,' prioritizing expansion and performance despite the personal cost.

rationales.self_direction: He turns down the corporate role 'obviously' and ends by affirming 'I don't owe anyone an explanation' for the life he chose.
rationales.achievement: He reacts to the slight by listing 'my clients, my revenue, my growth numbers,' showing a strong pull toward proving competence and success.

rationales.self_direction: He reframes flexibility as 'having the freedom to say no,' centering the choice on preserving a life that feels like his own.
rationales.stimulation: His 'immediate reaction was no' to an 'interesting project' because it would disrupt familiar routines, which resists novelty and change.
rationales.security: He is attached to 'Thursday dinners,' 'morning runs,' and the routines that make life here feel stable and like 'mine,' showing preference for continuity.

rationales.self_direction: He negotiates remote consulting instead of relocating and is praised for 'acting like someone who knows what he wants,' reflecting clearer self-authorship.
rationales.stimulation: He and Layla are planning 'another trip for March' to go 'somewhere new,' explicitly embracing novelty as part of the life he wants.
rationales.hedonism: He concludes 'The reward for good work isn't more work - it's more life,' directly prioritizing enjoyment over endless labor.

rationales.power: When Felix presents the preprocessing idea as his own, the writer 'didn't say anything,' yielding status and influence instead of asserting authorship.

rationales.self_direction: She rejects the prestigious placement because she refuses to spend two years on work that 'doesn't feel like mine' and says 'My project, my call.'
rationales.achievement: She wants 'something to show for it' and believes 'results shut down the lecture faster than arguments do,' emphasizing demonstrable competence.
rationales.security: Turning down Professor Hecht's placement means giving up the more established and credentialed path in favor of a riskier build-from-scratch route.

rationales.self_direction: She insists on 'building my own pipeline' and admits she keeps refusing help because she needs it 'to be entirely mine.'
rationales.achievement: That insistence is backfiring: she is 'already behind on coursework,' 'drowning quietly,' and has not started the fellowship statement before the deadline.
rationales.conformity: She cannot honestly frame herself as 'a team player' and describes working better alone, showing friction with collaborative expectations.

rationales.power: He fires Fadi because 'a company cannot have two captains' and fixates on preserving respect and keeping his 'grip' on the team.
rationales.tradition: He acts against the loyalty code his grandmother taught - that a man is nothing without the people who stood by him - by dismissing the cofounder who believed in him early on.
rationales.benevolence: He prioritizes authority over close relationships, letting go of Fadi despite their history and hanging up on Amira instead of staying in dialogue.

rationales.power: The investor keeps questioning whether he should even be CEO and smiles 'like I had said something charming,' undermining his standing and authority.
rationales.security: Questions about governance, founder stability, and whether he needs a more experienced CEO leave him feeling 'smaller' and shake confidence in the venture's stability.

rationales.self_direction: He experiences real control as 'control over myself' and over 'the kind of leader I am choosing to be,' emphasizing deliberate self-authorship.
rationales.benevolence: He drops the posturing, listens to Rami for two hours, and tells him his ideas are good, making space for someone close in his team to contribute.

rationales.self_direction: After being told to switch formats, she decides 'I'm not changing what's working,' backing her own classroom judgment.
rationales.conformity: She resists the coordinadora's directive instead of complying with the standardized framework expected by the school.

rationales.self_direction: She tells the coordinadora she will document progress 'however she needs' but 'won't gut the program to fit a checkbox,' defending her own teaching approach.
rationales.conformity: She openly refuses to 'comply and keep my head down,' pushing back against institutional expectations and pressure from her mom.
rationales.benevolence: Her resistance is grounded in care for students, pointing to Sofia finding her voice and insisting the workbook would hurt what helps the class.

rationales.tradition: The afternoon with abuela centers on familiar family practices - 'she'd made sancocho,' they watched a novela, and she notes the anniversary of abuelo's passing.
rationales.benevolence: She spends her Saturday helping Camilo study, fixes abuela's back door, and checks in on how she seems, showing sustained care for close family.

rationales.hedonism: After parent meetings she stays late to reorganize the library and ends up 'too fried to be useful,' signaling self-denial and depletion rather than enjoyment or ease.

rationales.self_direction: She decides to keep using 'my own plans' and submit the official template 'as a formality,' choosing her own judgment over imposed scripts.
rationales.conformity: Rather than 'just go along,' she deliberately works around the district mandate because she refuses to teach something she does not believe in.
rationales.benevolence: Her reasoning is centered on the kids in Ciudad Bolivar, saying they need someone 'paying attention to what actually reaches them' instead of generic worksheets.

rationales.hedonism: She is so overextended that she says 'There's only so much of me to go around,' with school demands and family obligations crowding out ease and recovery.
rationales.benevolence: She stays forty extra minutes to help Diego write to his father and reflects that many kids 'just need someone to stop and listen for half an hour.'

rationales.self_direction: She designs the reading unit around what students actually need, saying the best ideas come 'not from a planning document' but from paying attention to them.
rationales.tradition: Making 'arepas de choclo' with abuela while hearing stories about how she and abuelo made them on a clay stove reflects continuity with family heritage.
rationales.benevolence: She tutors Camilo, spends time with abuela because 'she seems to need to tell' her stories, and builds a class unit from Diego's needs.
